description_long: This sculpture depicts a seated figure in a meditative pose, with legs crossed and hands positioned in traditional mudras. The figure has a serene facial expression, elongated earlobes, and tightly curled hair, characteristic of many representations of the Buddha. The surface is weathered, with a dark, stone-like texture and subtle variations in color, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The figure sits atop a simple rectangular base, which appears to be made of the same material as the sculpture itself. The overall form is solid and compact, with well-defined anatomical features and a sense of calm and stability. The sculpture's proportions and details indicate careful craftsmanship and a focus on spiritual symbolism.
description: A weathered stone sculpture of a seated meditative figure with curled hair and elongated earlobes, resting on a rectangular base. The figure exudes calm and stability, with detailed features and a dark, textured surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face, crossed legs, and both hands in traditional mudras, highlighting the serene expression and detailed torso.
description_view_1: Left side view emphasizing the profile of the face, the elongated earlobe, and the arm positions, with the rectangular base clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the head with curled hair, the broad shoulders, and the back resting against the base.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the opposite profile, the hand gestures, and the leg positioning, with the base supporting the figure.
attribute_location_context: No specific installation context is visible; the sculpture is isolated against a plain background.
